feat: Cache account insights in Supabase to reduce Meta API calls (#59)

Add account_insights_cache table and caching layer to the insights
endpoint. Cached data is served for 24 hours before refreshing from
Meta. Supports ?refresh=true to force bypass cache.

Closes #23, part of #14

Remove implementation details that undermine service value

Event marketing page was exposing how we build (GitHub Pages, JSON
configs, zero hosting cost, static HTML) instead of selling what
we deliver. Prospects don't need our recipe.

Changes:
- Remove all GitHub Pages/zero-cost/static HTML references
- Remove JSON config file implementation details
- Reframe "Config-Driven Architecture" as "Launch New Cities in Hours"
- Rewrite "How We Build" section to focus on process, not tech stack
- Fix FAQ answers to stop explaining hosting infrastructure
- Replace "config-driven" with "reusable" across all references
- Swap outbound link from GitHub Pages to CMS NPI Registry

feat: add 10 threat intel source adapters with strict data validation

Sources added (all with rate limiting, timeouts, input sanitization):
- NVD (National Vulnerability Database) — 336K+ CVEs
- CISA KEV (Known Exploited Vulnerabilities) — confirmed exploited
- MITRE ATT&CK — 800+ adversary techniques
- Abuse.ch URLhaus — malware distribution URLs
- Abuse.ch MalwareBazaar — malware sample hashes
- Abuse.ch ThreatFox — IOCs linked to malware families
- OSV.dev — open source package vulnerabilities
- GitHub Advisory Database — package security advisories
- AlienVault OTX — community threat indicators
- ExploitDB — exploit code database

Security measures:
- DataValidator with 14 validation checks per record
- Input sanitization (XSS/injection stripping)
- Strict format validation (CVE, CWE, MITRE IDs, IP, hash, URL)
- Record freshness check (reject >365 days)
- SHA-256 content fingerprint deduplication
- String length limits (prevent memory abuse)
- CVSS range validation
- Indicator count limits

Architecture:
- Unified ThreatIntelRecord type normalizing all sources
- ThreatIntelAdapter interface for all sources
- ThreatIntelPipeline orchestrator (sequential or parallel)
- 100 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: mdns-adguard-sync — bridge mDNS to AdGuard Home DNS rewrites

Listens for mDNS service announcements on the local network and
syncs discovered hostname->IP mappings to AdGuard Home via REST API.

Replaces mDNS reflectors (which cause multicast storms) with clean
unicast DNS that works across subnets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(extension): SSE streaming generation UI (#88)

* feat(extension): streaming SSE generation with token-by-token UI

- Add SSE event types (meta, token, done, error) to shared/types.ts
- Add parseSSEStream() utility for manual SSE parsing with fetch+getReader
- Add apiClient.generateStream() method (POST /generate with stream:true)
- Rewrite useGenerateReply hook for streaming with ref-based token accumulation
- Update ReplyPanel: live text during streaming, cursor animation, disabled
  Copy/Insert buttons, Stop button with aria-label="Stop generating"
- Error mid-stream: preserve partial text + inline error message
- a11y: aria-live="polite" + role="log" on streaming container, screen
  reader announcements on completion/error, disabled attributes on buttons
- Add streaming cursor CSS with blink animation + prefers-reduced-motion
- 24 new tests: SSE parser (7), api-client stream (3), hook streaming (14)
- All 233 tests pass, typecheck + lint clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(extension): address review findings (1 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 2 LOW)

- [H1] RAF throttle: tokens accumulate in ref, setReply called via
  requestAnimationFrame (batched at ~16ms) instead of per-token setState
- [M1] Add comment explaining InsertButton self-disabling via isGenerating
- [M2] Add reader.cancel() before releaseLock() in SSE parser cleanup
- [M3] Consolidate duplicate placeholder blocks into single conditional
- [M4] Set partial lastResponse on SSE error so context docs are preserved
- [L1] Add comment explaining 0.8s cursor blink vs 120ms transition standard
- [L3] Fix stream:false in api-client-stream test base request

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Redesign UI with ColorBrewer2 Set2 palette and UX improvements

Complete visual overhaul:

  blue (#8da0cb), pink (#e78ac3), lime (#a6d854), gold (#ffd92f)

- Smoother transitions and better typography
- PRD output and Word export updated to match
- Role colors and settings swatches updated to Set2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(backend): SSE streaming, exception handlers, ChromaDB warm-up (#89)

* feat(backend): SSE streaming endpoint, global exception handlers, ChromaDB warm-up

Sprint A backend deliverables:

1. SSE Streaming Endpoint
   - Add LLMService.generate_stream() async generator for token-by-token streaming
   - Add streaming branch to POST /generate (when stream=true)
   - SSE event format: meta (context_docs) → token* → done (latency_ms)
   - Error mid-stream emits error SSE event instead of breaking the connection
   - Keep existing non-streaming JSON response unchanged (dual endpoint)

2. Global Exception Handlers + Typed Errors
   - Add ErrorCode enum (LLM_DOWN, MODEL_ERROR, INTERNAL_ERROR) and ErrorResponse model
   - Register ConnectionError → 503 and LLMModelError → 502 handlers on the app
   - Add UnhandledExceptionMiddleware (ASGI) for generic 500 catch-all
   - Remove local try/except blocks in generate router (now handled globally)

3. ChromaDB Cold-Start Warm-up
   - Add warmup_chromadb() called during lifespan startup
   - Calls .get_collection() + .count() on kb_articles and whd_tickets
   - Gracefully handles missing collections (debug log, no crash)

4. Tests (+18 new tests, 413 total)
   - 8 SSE streaming tests (happy path, error mid-stream, empty response,
     context docs in meta, non-streaming regression, headers, logging)
   - 4 exception handler tests (503, 502, 500 catch-all, response shape)
   - 3 ChromaDB warm-up tests (counts, missing, partial)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(backend): address review findings (2 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 1 LOW)

[H1] Streaming callers get SSE error (not JSON 503) when _prepare_context fails
[H2] UnhandledExceptionMiddleware logs warning when response already started
[M1] Use self._client.base_url instead of settings.llm_base_url in LLMService
[M2] Restore web_docs count in non-streaming generate log
[M3] Assert specific collection names in warmup partial test
[M4] Catch generic Exception in _stream_generate, yield INTERNAL_ERROR SSE event
[L3] Move lazy KB_COLLECTION/TICKET_COLLECTION import to top-level in main.py

Adds 2 new tests: context prep failure → SSE error, generic exception → SSE INTERNAL_ERROR
415 tests pass, ruff clean, mypy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

  integration, and Make.com webhook triggers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: crypto migration to AEAD (AES-256-GCM) with HLE1 v3 (T-010) (#12)

* feat: crypto migration to AEAD (AES-256-GCM) with HLE1 v3 (T-010)

- Add HLE1 v3 format with 28-byte header, AEAD (AES-256-GCM),
  HKDF-SHA256 key derivation (RFC 5869), and algorithm ID field
- Replace custom SHA-256 counter-mode stream cipher + external
  HMAC-SHA256 MAC with standard AEAD for new encryptions
- Add `cryptography>=43.0` as optional `crypto` extra; fall back
  to v2 format when package is unavailable
- Maintain full backward read compatibility for HLE1 v1/v2
- Update FORMAT.md (v3 spec), THREAT_MODEL.md, DESIGN.md (spec-first)
- Add comprehensive v3 tests: roundtrip, tamper detection, header
  validation, HKDF determinism, empty plaintext, cross-version compat

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* improve: simplify crypto code after review (T-010)

- Extract _scrypt_base_key helper to deduplicate scrypt calls
- Narrow except Exception to except InvalidTag in _decrypt_aead
- Eliminate double struct.unpack in validate; parse magic+version
  first, then full header per version
- Consolidate scrypt_n minimum check after version branches
- Fix salt length hardcode (use len(salt) instead of literal 16)
- Add algo_id guard in _decrypt_v3 for unsupported algorithms
- Bound ciphertext slice with info.ciphertext_len
- Unify reserved field error messages for v3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

resort to prevent OOM kills.

fix(android): trim trailing slash from baseUrl to prevent double-slash

takeFrom("http://x/api/v1/") creates empty trailing segment, causing
appendPathSegments to produce /api/v1//auth/login (404). trimEnd('/')
before takeFrom fixes this: /api/v1/auth/login (correct).

v10.1.2144: TS-PASS-ERP-CONTEXT-TO-SCOPE-ROUTER-LLM-V3.1 — pass routing context to scope router LLM

Scope router LLM now receives recent ERP context (last action, entity type,
entity name, time since mutation) as a second system message. This allows
the LLM to correctly classify short follow-up messages like "sorta je
solkanski regut" as ERP when they continue an active ERP flow.

S3 prompt updated with CONVERSATION CONTEXT section instructing the LLM
to use context for follow-up interpretation while preserving ADVISORY
override for genuine advisory questions.

feat(validators): add invariants checker for file content requirements

Adds a new `invariants` validator that checks any file in the project
contains a required substring. Configured via `invariants:` in sentinel.yaml.

Used to prevent regressions like UILaunchScreen disappearing from iOS
Info.plist (which letterboxes the app on modern iPhones with no runtime fix).

security/ (11 files):

- All types prefixed with security. in main/server imports
- tenantRateLimiter moved back to ratelimit.go (depends on main pkg)

cluster/ (16 files):
- distributed.go, coordinator.go, shard.go, replication.go, follower.go,
  election.go, quorum.go, rebalance.go, snapshot.go, failover.go,
  wal_stream.go, wal_integration.go, types.go + 3 tests
- Interface-based design (Store, Embedder, MetricsProvider, AuthProvider)
- Deps struct for dependency injection from main

main.go changes:
- Remove runCoordinatorMode/runShardMode (experimental, use cluster pkg)
- Remove replication setup block (was only used by cluster code)
- Remove VectorStore.replicationMgr/replicas fields

Root: 46 Go files → 31 Go files
